entries.title: Always provide --dry-run for destructive CLI operations
Include 'when to use' guidance in --help output
entries.description:
# Include 'when to use' guidance in --help output

## Context

CLI help text should explain not just WHAT flags do, but WHEN to use them

## Pattern

Add a 'When to use' section after listing options that provides guidance on which flag to use in which scenario.

## Example

```typescript
When to use each flag:
  --dry-run      Use FIRST to see what will change
  --interactive  Use for guided cleanup with prompts
  --cleanup      Use in CI/automation for direct action
```

## Why

Users often don't know which flag combination they need. 'When to use' guidance reduces trial-and-error and gets them to the right command faster.

entries.title: Reload Intelligence After Vault Updates
entries.description:
# Reload Intelligence After Vault Updates

## Context

After modifying intelligence JSON files in the vault

## Pattern

The MCP server caches intelligence at startup. After updating vault data files, call salvador_reload_intelligence() to refresh the cache without restarting the server.

## Example

```typescript
// After editing workflow-patterns.json in vault:
salvador_reload_intelligence()
// Returns: { success: true, reloadTime: '9ms', ... }
```

## Why

The singleton pattern caches intelligence for performance. Without reload, changes to vault files won't be visible until server restart.

entries.title: README should defer to --help for CLI documentation
entries.description:
# README should defer to --help for CLI documentation

## Context

CLI tool documentation in README.md should be minimal and point to --help

## Pattern

Instead of duplicating all flags in README, show 1-2 common examples and add 'Run --help for complete documentation'. This prevents drift between README and actual CLI behavior.

## Example

```typescript
```bash
# Preview what will be exported
npm run export-hooks /path --dry-run

# See all options
npm run export-hooks -- --help
```

> Note: Run --help for complete, up-to-date documentation.
```

## Why

Documentation in multiple places (README, --help, CLAUDE.md) inevitably drifts. Single source of truth in --help ensures accuracy.

entries.title: Warn about unknown flags instead of failing silently
Silently ignoring unknown CLI flags
entries.description:
# Silently ignoring unknown CLI flags

## Context

CLI scripts that store unknown flags but never warn about them

## Anti-Pattern

Tracking unknown flags in an array but never displaying a warning means users think features are enabled when they're not.

## Example (Bad)

```typescript
// BAD:
else if (arg.startsWith('--')) {
  args.unknownFlags.push(arg); // Stored but never warned
}
```

## Why It's Wrong

User types --with-herald-company (wrong), script stores it but continues silently. User thinks Herald is configured with custom company when it's using default.

## Correct Approach

See: [unknown-flag-warning](../../patterns/other/unknown-flag-warning.md)

entries.title: Anteater Generation Outputs
entries.description:
# Anteater Generation Outputs

## Context

When explaining what Anteater generates or checking if a project is Anteater-generated

## Pattern

Anteater generates: .anteater.json (project config), tailwind-preset.js (OKLCH tokens 50-900), .claude/ directory (49 skills, 40+ hookify rules, agent-context.json), .mcp.json (points to salvador-mcp), src/components/ui/ (empty, add via 'anteater add'), tokens.css and base.css (CSS custom properties).

## Example

```typescript
# Check if Anteater project
if [ -f ".anteater.json" ] && [ -f ".claude/agent-context.json" ]; then
  echo "This is an Anteater project"
fi
```

## Why

Understanding Anteater's output structure helps Salvador validate projects and guide users to the correct files for customization.

entries.title: Soleri Development Principle: Salvador is the Reference Implementation
entries.description:
# Soleri Development Principle: Salvador is the Reference Implementation

## Context

Established during Cognee integration work when feature audit revealed massive gap between generated agents and Salvador capabilities.

## Principle

Every engine-level feature in Salvador MCP should exist in every Soleri-generated agent. Salvador's code is the blueprint — port features from Salvador into @soleri/core and @soleri/forge, don't reinvent. Generated agents must be Salvador-grade (minus domain-specific design system intelligence). Curator, brain intelligence pipeline, loops, orchestration, identity, governance — all engine features. Currently generated agents have ~36 ops across 2 facades; Salvador has 181+ ops across 14 facades. See GitHub milestones v5.1–v7.0.

## Example

N/A

## Why

Without this principle, Soleri agents ship as toy demos instead of production-grade knowledge management platforms. Salvador has battle-tested every feature; porting is faster and more reliable than reimplementing.
